channel.title: Welcome weary traveler
channel.description: Recent content on Welcome weary traveler

item.title: Resume Website
item.description: Take a gander at the source behind this very site and the program that generates my resume from a simple .yaml file.

item.title: Snake
item.description: Simple snake game built using HTML5 canvas and Rust’s web assembly support. It was definitely more work than just writing JS by hand, and I doubt it’s any faster. That said, it was a very fun project, and I wouldn’t mind similar work in future. I can see Rust WASM being useful in another project when you care about correctness, or some large project where you care about maintenance costs.

item.title: gtaggenator
item.description: I’ve long wondered if we could improve on the traditional hierarchical file system. It’s definitely been attempted before, many times (see this wiki page). gtaggenator, a CLI written in go, is my take on it.

item.title: Face Game
item.description: I wanted to play around with modern JavaScript facial recognition, which seems to be face-api.js. It was striking to me just how easy it was to get going. From requesting a camera feed to asking the library for the user’s most likely expression, the whole project was very smooth.

item.title: Spooky Face Game
item.description: Another foray into facial recognition with face-api.js, this time with a halloween twist.

item.title: Secret Santa
item.description: Simple client project to help me manage my yearly remote secret santas. Very similar in concept to this project, but with some extra features. Includes import/export functionality for if you play with the same group multiple years and don’t want duplicate pairings.

item.title: Tetris
item.description: Bare-bones tetris clone built using the snake game as a template. It was very fast to make, in part because so much of the boilerplate was done, but also because Rust’s advanced type system makes refactoring a breeze.

item.title: Conception
item.description: This site was made, primarily, as a vehicle for my résumé. It could have been a one-day thing. Just find a pretty wordpress template, customize it a bit, and voilà! But where’s the fun in that? I mean, what self-respecting dev doesn’t love a good greenfield project. So, to justify this waste of time, I came up with some requirements: Free This was by far the easiest task since github will host a page for your user.

item.title: What's with the name?
item.description: “loremdipso”? What’s that? Did you mean lorem ipsum? Or, ipso facto maybe? Ah, well, let me take you back to the summer of 1992, when I was born. The story I’ve been told is that my sister wanted to name me after that kid on the Kix commercial. You know, the one that goes “Mikey likes it!"1. Pretty cute, right? Named by my sister, who was only 6 or 7, after a silly cereal commercial.
